name: Cajun Shrimp Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound raw medium prawns, 18 to 24 prawns
1/2 cup yellow onion, peeled and chopped
seeded, pits removed, and coarsely chopped
seeded, pits removed, and coarsely chopped
1 1/2 cups fish stock or bottled clam juice
2 tablespoons cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup water
1/4 cup Italian parsley, stemmed and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook rice first, and while it is cooking, cook shrimp. To cook shrimp, bring water to a boil; add shrimp and boil for 6-8 minutes until color changes to pink. Rinse in cold water (to stop cooking), and drain.
2. Prepare the stew by squeezing juice of half lemon over cooked shrimp and setting aside.
3. Heat oil in a 6-quart saucepan on medium heat. Cook onion until softened and translucent, about 6-8 minutes.
4. Add garlic, chopped peppers, celery, thyme (fresh or dried), and file seasoning. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
5. Stir in tomatoes, wine, crushed red pepper flakes, shrimp cooking liquid, fish stock, paprika, Worcestershire sauce, and salt. Simmer until flavors meld, about 10 minutes. Taste and adjust the level of heat from the peppers if needed.
6. If thickening is desired, stir in the cornstarch mixture (cornstarch mixed with water) and simmer for an additional 3-4 minutes.
7. Stir in chopped parsley and set aside until ready to serve. Just before serving, add cooked shrimp that has been heated through. Squeeze remaining lemon juice over the shrimp and serve.
